briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for July 22, 2026. Based on 197 climate-related news items collected globally and domestically today, I will provide a detailed report on the severity of the climate crisis we currently face, the various efforts being made to address it, and significant advancements for the future.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Economic Repercussions
sections.content: Today's news warns of the globally deepening climate crisis and its widespread economic and social repercussions. In Europe, 'S-fear,' an economic concern stemming from heatwaves, has emerged [7, 183], while severe wildfires in Canada are issuing air quality alerts across multiple U.S. states and worsening air quality in Philadelphia, demonstrating the reality of cross-border climate disasters [176, 185, 196]. Domestically, an increase in greenhouse gases is accelerating rice plant growth but degrading rice yield and quality, raising serious concerns for future food security [11, 28]. Furthermore, research indicating that climate change could shorten the lifespan of early-generation electric vehicle batteries by up to 30% reveals the vulnerability across industries [43, 60]. Direct impacts of climate change are also being reported in various parts of the world, including food insecurity in small island regions, intensifying drought in Colorado, and the shrinking of the Caspian Sea, which are increasing health risks for children and the elderly and putting millions in the UK at risk of coastal flooding [175, 177, 190, 191, 192].

sections.title: 🌱 Policy Progress and Strengthening Carbon Neutrality Goals
sections.content: Amidst this crisis, there is active progress in policy and efforts to strengthen carbon neutrality goals in response to the climate crisis. Today, the National Assembly's Special Committee on Climate passed an amendment to the Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality, achieving a significant milestone by legislating greenhouse gas reduction targets of 69-80% by 2040 and 84-90% by 2045 [42, 61, 102, 173]. This amendment specifies concrete reduction pathways from 2031 to 2049 and reflects the Constitutional Court's decision [80, 83, 84], demonstrating a clearer commitment to implementation by stipulating upper and lower limits for greenhouse gas reduction targets in law [26]. International cooperation efforts continue, with South Korea and India discussing strengthening collaboration in sustainability sectors such as climate change, oceans, and the Arctic [1]. Climate Minister Kim Sung-hwan also stated that he would support enhancing the global green competitiveness of Korean companies through on-site initiatives to promote carbon neutrality and the circular economy in the industrial sector [69, 98, 108, 126, 137]. The expansion of professional training in preparation for mandatory ESG disclosures and companies' plans to establish ESG committees and publish reports indicate responsible changes in the private sector [18, 41, 51].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Community Adaptation Efforts and Technological Innovation
sections.content: News of community adaptation efforts and technological innovations in response to climate change also continued. Iksan City has significantly expanded and begun managing landslide-prone areas in preparation for climate change [2, 47]. Yongin Special City is encouraging citizen-participatory adaptation activities by recruiting participants for its summer climate crisis adaptation program, 'The Most Exciting Summer to Save the Earth: Climate Crisis Adaptation Operation' [6, 29, 31, 39, 45, 48, 53, 56]. Gyeongsangnam-do selected 29 pilot aquaculture sites for climate change response, achieving the highest number nationwide [32]. Seosan City is enhancing agricultural climate resilience by distributing 'Geumseon Potato,' a tissue-cultured variety resistant to climate change, for the first time [16, 36, 64]. Furthermore, the Incheon Institute has signed a joint research agreement with global water company DHI in water resource management and climate change adaptation, striving to establish urban water management and safety nets [12, 30, 37, 44]. The Korea Institute of Machinery and Materials (KIMM) is actively pursuing technological innovation, including developing an eco-friendly and highly efficient integrated conversion solution that can reduce carbon emissions from industrial heat supply systems by 44% [99, 118].

sections.title: 🌍 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: The government is accelerating its policy drive to achieve the 100GW renewable energy target [139], implementing market revitalization measures such as introducing self-consumption renewable energy certificates and allowing the sale of residential solar electricity [22, 143, 147, 152, 155, 163]. Green transition investments are also active in the financial and industrial sectors, with Shinhan Bank Vietnam expanding loans for renewable energy projects and S-OIL commencing the supply of bio-marine fuel, targeting the carbon-neutral market in the shipping industry [112, 140]. KEPCO KDN is leading the distributed power generation ecosystem through the establishment of public-interest renewable energy Special Purpose Companies (SPCs) and public-led solar power projects [141, 156, 159]. Korea East-West Power (EWP) is contributing to enhancing grid acceptance of renewable energy through its AI-based Energy Storage System (ESS) construction project [150]. However, concerns are also being raised that the surge in power grid demand due to the AI boom and the variability of renewable energy have put the 2026 carbon neutrality target at risk [115, 145]. Efforts to address these challenges are being pursued concurrently, such as exploring solutions for grid stabilization amidst the rapid increase in renewable energy through the launch of the Grid Code Expert Committee [169, 172].

sections.title: 🌱 Citizen Participation and Expansion of Climate Education
sections.content: Educational and participatory programs aimed at increasing public understanding of climate change and encouraging action are expanding nationwide. The Jeonju Meteorological Administration is operating 'Visiting Climate Change Experience Education' for climate-vulnerable groups [9, 13, 20]. The Wonju Climate Change Promotion Center and Yongin City are actively conducting carbon neutrality and climate crisis adaptation experience education for elementary school students and citizens during the summer vacation [14, 24, 25, 35, 40, 46, 50, 53, 58, 59]. Notably, with the ability to donate Carbon Neutrality Points to the Community Chest of Korea starting in August [65, 70, 71, 74, 78, 79, 85, 89, 94, 111, 124, 131], a new donation culture is being fostered where green lifestyle practices lead to neighborly love. Kookmin University's HUSS Climate Change Response Project Group is strengthening the climate solution capabilities of future generations by conducting field studies on renewable energy and desertification response in China [5, 33, 62]. The Gyeongbuk Provincial Office of Education is enhancing the expertise of climate education in schools by providing carbon neutrality job training for teachers [81, 109, 134].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has once again highlighted the severity of the global climate crisis, while also demonstrating that multifaceted efforts—policy-driven, technological, and citizen-participatory—are underway to overcome it. Continued attention and action are crucial for responding to climate change, and I will continue to deliver updates on these developments in depth. This concludes the climate briefing for July 22, 2026. Thank you.
